title: Timeseries
content:
# Timeseries

Inherits from: ApiServiceModel


## Methods


### create_timeseries(publish: Optional[bool], data: Optional[Union[requests.TimeseriesCreate, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> None

Create Time Series Data individually or in bulk for one or more Asset / Data Stream resources.


### get_timeseries_last(data: Optional[Union[requests.TimeseriesLastGet, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> KList[responses.TimeseriesLastGet]

Returns a list of Time Series objects and its latest value. The list returned must be filtered by one or more `resources` and optionally on `fields`. This list is not pageable.


### list_timeseries(pagination_type: Optional[Literal[limits, cursor, stream]], page_size: Optional[int], page: Optional[int], next: Optional[str], previous: Optional[str], direction: Optional[Literal[asc, desc]], sort_by: Optional[Sequence[str]], data: Optional[Union[requests.TimeseriesList, Mapping[str, object]]], fetch: bool, _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Union[KList[type.TimeseriesData], responses.TimeseriesListPaginatedResponseCursor]

Returns a list of Time Series objects and its latest value. The list returned can be optionally restricted to one or more resources and/or sources.


### download_timeseries_range(data: Optional[Union[requests.TimeseriesRangeDownload, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> Iterator[None]

Returns a **CSV file** with Time Series data within the specified time range one or more resources (Asset /Data Stream pairs). Optional to preprocess and aggregate the data on the server using `agg` and `time_bucket` before downloading.


### get_timeseries_range(data: Optional[Union[requests.TimeseriesRangeGet, Mapping[str, object]]], _dry_run: bool, _client: Optional[SyncBaseClient]) -> KIterator[responses.TimeseriesRangeGet]

Returns an array with Time Series data within the specified time range for one or more resources (Asset /Data Stream pairs). Optional to preprocess and aggregate the data on the server using `agg` and `time_bucket` before downloading.

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client()
result = client.client.api.timeseries.create_timeseries()
